framework.name: OAuth Assumes Single Actor Per Request
framework.description: The article explicitly states that OAuth's single-subject model breaks when agents delegate to other agents. This confirms the gap but doesn't solve it — the OBO pattern still anchors everything to the original human user.
framework.raw_quote: Most identity systems assume there's only one actor per request. That model breaks as soon as you introduce agents

source.title: Understanding "On-Behalf-Of" in AI agent authentication - Scalekit
